description: Remove IDJ (Issue Detection Justification) dimension from v5 scoring engine and all plan files
rationale: IDJ was designed for the old memo-format cases where task prompts contained wrong justifications (addressed_but_incorrectly metadata). The ARCH-1 pipeline redesign replaced memos with experiment designs — flaws are planted in methodology, not defended with wrong reasoning. New pipeline cases have no addressed_but_incorrectly metadata, so IDJ would be N/A for every case. The experiment-design format aligns more closely with actual ml-lab use (user proposes experiment, critic finds flaws, defender argues back). Wrong justifications come from the defender agent during debate, not from the task prompt. IDJ signal is already captured indirectly by FVC and DRQ.
alternatives: Keep IDJ and add addressed_but_incorrectly metadata to pipeline cases (rejected: reverts to memo-style, misaligns with ml-lab use). Keep IDJ as-is accepting N/A on all cases (rejected: vestigial dimension in fair-comparison dims adds confusion without measurement value).

description: Moved multi-agent-prompt.md from repo root to self_debate_experiment/multi-agent-prompt.md
rationale: The file is the canonical bootstrap prompt that produced the v1 experiment artifacts (not a draft), so it belongs at the experiment root alongside the artifacts it generated. The existing prompt-drafts/ subfolder retains earlier iterations that were not executed; placing the final prompt there would misrepresent its status.

Updates all cross-references across 6 files and reinstalls the plugin.

description: Redesigned the ml-journal plugin's synthesis skill layer: renamed research-note to research-report to better reflect its end-of-project scope, and created a new lightweight research-note skill for session or day-scoped formatted markdown notes. The new research-note checks conversation context for an existing log-summarize output first (reusing already-synthesized content) and falls back to querying journal entries directly. Output is RESEARCH_NOTE_<date>.md at repo root, 40-80 lines, with omit-if-empty sections. All cross-references updated across 6 files. Plugin reinstalled.
readme-rewriter is an ml-lab workflow agent, not a standalone skill

message: docs: overhaul ml-journal README with install, workflow, examples, and troubleshooting
diff_summary: Third-party agent review identified missing installation instructions, no workflow examples, absent output samples, missing enum values, inaccurate file tree, and no troubleshooting. README rewritten to address all gaps: added Installation, Typical Workflow, Example Output, Weight column and enum footnotes in entry types table, direct query examples in Scripts, Hooks gotchas, Troubleshooting section, and accurate Files tree.

description: Keep proactive logging triggers as independent patterns without explicit chaining rules
rationale: The trigger patterns already cover each link in the chain independently (issue, resolution, lesson each fire on their own signals). Explicit chaining would double the CLAUDE.md section size for behavior that emerges naturally. The one necessary rule (propose chain at completion, not three interruptions) is already included. Will add chaining only if Claude misses follow-ups in practice.
alternatives: Add explicit chaining logic mapping each entry type to its follow-up type (rejected: over-specifies, bloats CLAUDE.md)

description: Proactive journal logging plan — add CLAUDE.md behavioral instructions and update log-init to offer injection
key_decisions: Six entry types auto-proposed (decision
discovery
issue
resolution
lesson

experiment); all others stay user-initiated. Always ask before logging — no invisible execution. Independent trigger patterns without explicit chaining rules. log-init skill updated to offer CLAUDE.md injection during setup. research-note renamed to research-report; new research-note skill is session-scoped.

description: Root README does not list or document project-level skills (/artifact-sync, /preflight, /new-issue, /sync-ml-lab-docs, etc.)
context: Checked README.md — only /ml-lab slash command and ml-journal skills are referenced; no dedicated section covers the full set of available project-level skills

description: Write normalize_cases.py to transform ARCH-1 pipeline output schema → experiment schema
rationale: Keeps pipeline output pristine; experiment scripts (validate_cases.py, self_debate_poc.py, filter_verified_cases.py) remain stable — safer than updating scattered field accesses across all experiment scripts
alternatives: Update all experiment scripts to accept the flat pipeline schema directly (more edits, higher risk of missing a callsite in self_debate_poc.py)

description: ARCH-1 pipeline output uses flat schema; experiment scripts expect nested schema
context: Inspecting selected_cases_all.json (110 cases) before Phase 0. Fields category, difficulty, ground_truth.correct_position, scoring_targets.*, ideal_debate_resolution all absent or differently structured. validate_cases.py crashes immediately on c['category'].

description: selected_cases_all.json contains 0 mixed cases (80 critique, 30 defense_wins); validate_cases.py and filter_verified_cases.py require >= 12 and >= 10 mixed cases respectively
context: ARCH-1 two-node pipeline produces binary verdicts only — cases are either corrupted (critique) or sound (defense_wins). The mixed/empirical_test_agreed category does not exist in this design. Both validation scripts hard-assert on mixed count.

description: Pipeline must_not_claim is a list of narrative dicts {claim, why_wrong}; self_debate_poc.py expects a list of ID strings for set-membership testing
context: compute_idp() does 'if i in must_not_claim' where i is a raised issue ID string. Pipeline dicts never match strings — IDP scoring silently returns wrong results. Fix: assign synthetic IDs mnc_001..N; preserve narratives in must_not_claim_details.

description: Pipeline acceptable_resolutions are resolution-description dicts (resolution_id, description, supports_critique_if, supports_defense_if); scoring_targets.acceptable_resolutions must be verdict-type strings (critique_wins, defense_wins) for FVC/DRQ computation
context: compute_fvc() and compute_drq() do 'if verdict in acceptable_resolutions' where verdict is a string like 'critique_wins'. Pipeline dicts never satisfy this. Fix: remap to list of verdict-type strings; preserve pipeline dicts in resolution_descriptions.

description: Added ## Project Skills section to root README documenting all 4 project-local slash commands
approach: Appended a new section after the ml-journal section with a 4-row table (/artifact-sync, /new-issue, /preflight, /sync-ml-lab-docs) and a cross-reference line to /ml-lab and ml-journal skills

description: Split the research-report skill into a skill + agent pair following the new-issue → issue-drafter pattern. Created report-drafter.md agent at the plugin root (model: sonnet, color: cyan) to handle all heavy context ingestion (full journal, git log, supplementary docs) in an isolated subcontext. Rewrote research-report/SKILL.md to dispatch the agent rather than synthesize inline. Added agents array to plugin.json (version bumped to 0.2.0). Updated README with Agents table and Files tree. Updated journal_system_spec.md and slides. Discussed why this pattern fits research-report (full-history reads) but not research-note (scoped, lighter) or log-entry/checkpoint (depend on main conversation context).

message: feat: split research-report into skill + report-drafter agent
diff_summary: Extracts heavy context ingestion from research-report into a dedicated report-drafter subagent (plugin root, registered in plugin.json). The skill now dispatches the agent rather than reading the full journal, git log, and supplementary docs inline. Plugin version bumped to 0.2.0.

description: Phase 1 Check 10 (red herring validation) broken by schema normalization: scoring_targets.must_not_claim is now opaque IDs (mnc_001..N), not claim descriptions; agent cannot validate red-herring content from IDs alone
context: normalize_cases.py maps pipeline must_not_claim dicts to synthetic ID strings for scorer compatibility. Phase 1 agent prompt references must_not_claim for Check 10 but descriptions are now in must_not_claim_details — a field the prompt does not mention. Agent will see non-empty ID list and consider Check 10 trivially satisfied without reading content.

description: Phase 1 Check 9 (hard domain expertise) weakened: prompt references ideal_critique which is [] for all ARCH-1 cases; agent has no expertise signal to read from that field
context: ARCH-1 pipeline does not generate ideal_critique or ideal_defense — set to empty lists in normalize_cases.py. Phase 1 Check 9 tells agent to use ideal_critique for domain expertise signals; with empty lists agent must infer from task_prompt and planted_issues but prompt does not redirect it there.

description: Phase 1 Check 12 (difficulty label validation) will produce spurious REVISE verdicts: agent re-judges difficulty by inspection, conflicting with empirical proxy_mean labels
context: Difficulty labels in benchmark_cases.json are derived from _pipeline.proxy_mean (Sonnet smoke test scores). Check 12 instructs the agent to independently assess difficulty and mark REVISE if 'a hard case's flaw is findable by standard single-pass reasoning' — which will disagree with empirical labels on borderline cases, undoing calibrated proxy scores.

description: Phase 1 Check 10 fixed: agent prompt redirected to must_not_claim_details for claim descriptions
approach: Updated Check 10 in phase_01_case_verifier.md to read scoring_targets.must_not_claim_details ({id, claim, why_wrong} dicts) instead of opaque ID strings. Agent can now verify red-herring content against task_prompt.

description: Phase 1 Check 9 fixed: agent prompt redirected to planted_issues and task_prompt instead of ideal_critique
approach: Updated Check 9 to note ideal_critique is empty for ARCH-1 and to use planted_issues[].description + task_prompt to assess domain expertise requirement.

description: Phase 1 Check 12 fixed: difficulty labels treated as empirically authoritative; agent only flags gross inconsistencies
approach: Check 12 updated: labels from _pipeline.proxy_mean are authoritative. Agent marks REVISE only when label is grossly inconsistent with task_prompt, not as a re-judgment exercise. Proxy thresholds documented in prompt.

message: docs: regenerate ml-journal slides PDF
diff_summary: Regenerated slides/ml-journal-overview.pdf from Marp source using npx @marp-team/marp-cli

description: Python controls iteration, not prompts — for any multi-case pipeline stage, the Python script wraps the LLM call in a loop or executor; the prompt receives exactly one case and returns output for that case
context: Discovered during v5 synthetic case generation pipeline development when iteration logic was embedded in a prompt, causing non-deterministic batching behavior
applies_to: multi-case pipeline stages, LLM orchestration scripts, any stage processing N cases

message: chore: migrate lessons.md to project journal and delete file
diff_summary: Deleted tasks/lessons.md after migrating its single lesson (Python controls iteration, not prompts) to the project journal as a typed lesson entry (00537269). The journal is now the canonical home for lessons.

description: phase_05_5: difficulty gate requires 5 easy cases; ARCH-1 has 0 easy cases — gate cannot execute
context: Phase 5.5 selects a stratified pilot of '5 easy, 5 medium, 5 hard'. benchmark_cases.json has 42 hard, 68 medium, 0 easy. The gate cannot be constructed and the easy-stratum decision branch (proceed/stop/relabel) has no inputs.

description: phase_05_5: Spearman rank scheme uses easy=1 (nonexistent) and references 15-case pilot; with 0 easy cases degrades to 2-level point-biserial on 10 cases
context: Spearman check encodes easy=1, medium=2, hard=3 across 15 cases. No easy cases exist. Must change to medium=1, hard=2 on 10-case pilot.

description: phase_02: pre-registered 3-stratum analysis includes 'mixed' stratum with 0 cases; mixed stratum DRQ prediction references empirical_test_agreed which does not exist as a ground-truth type
context: Lines 47-51 of phase_02_hypothesis.md. ARCH-1 benchmark has 0 mixed cases. The mixed-stratum bullet predicts DRQ lift via empirical_test_agreed verdict — a ground-truth type not present in the dataset. Pre-registration will encode untestable hypotheses.

description: cross_model_scorer.py feeds opaque must_not_claim IDs to external model prompt; external model cannot evaluate false positives without claim descriptions
context: Line 46-58: script uses scoring_targets.must_not_claim (['mnc_001',...]) in prompt section 'ISSUES THAT WOULD BE FALSE POSITIVES IF RAISED'. IDs carry no semantic meaning. must_not_claim_details has the actual {id, claim, why_wrong} content. IDP cross-validation is non-functional as written. Also includes 'mixed' as a valid verdict option.

description: write_preregistration.py pre-registers secondary_ensemble_mixed hypothesis and 3-stratum breakdown including mixed — both untestable with ARCH-1 dataset
context: Lines 21-24: secondary_ensemble_mixed tests debate vs ensemble on mixed cases (0 exist). Lines 33-37: stratum_fc_lift lists mixed as a stratum. Pre-registration document will encode claims that cannot be evaluated.

description: stats_analysis.py produces hollow mixed_position output block (n=0, all None) in stats_results.json
context: Line 74 filters for correct_position=='mixed' (returns []). Lines 115-119 write a mixed_position section unconditionally. Output will contain {n:0, isolated_fair_mean: null, ensemble_fair_mean: null} — a hollow section that downstream coherence audits and report writers may query expecting data.

description: phase_01_case_verifier.md Checks 5 and 8 reference empirical_test_agreed and mixed-position cases respectively — both vacuously skipped under ARCH-1
context: Check 5 validates empirical_test_agreed ETD fields; Check 8 validates mixed-position two-sidedness. With 0 cases of either type, both checks silently do nothing. Not a blocker but misleading dead code.

description: cross_model_scorer.py: replaced opaque must_not_claim IDs with human-readable must_not_claim_details in prompt; formatted as '- [id] claim (why it is NOT a flaw: ...)'; removed 'mixed' from verdict options; updated prompt header from 'ISSUES THAT WOULD BE FALSE POSITIVES IF RAISED' to 'DESIGN CHOICES THAT ARE VALID'

description: stats_analysis.py: wrapped mixed_position output block in dict-unpacking conditional (**{...} if len(mixed) > 0 else {}); mixed filter remains in place (returns [] for ARCH-1); no mixed_position key emitted in stats_results.json when 0 mixed cases
approach: Edited stats_analysis.py output dict construction; used ** dict-unpacking for conditional key inclusion

description: Fixed is_resolved check in journal_query.py: linked_issue_id stores 8-char prefix but issue id is full UUID; prefix matching now used instead of exact set membership

message: fix: use prefix matching for resolved-issue detection in journal_query.py
diff_summary: Fixed silent bug where issues with resolutions were still showing as unresolved. linked_issue_id stores 8-char prefixes; issue ids are full UUIDs — exact set membership never matched. Added is_resolved() prefix helper and updated both cmd_status and cmd_unresolved_issues. Unresolved count dropped from 18 to 5.

description: Keep Phase 5.5 difficulty gate for v5 run despite ARCH-1 empirical difficulty labels
rationale: Static checks (normalize_cases.py, validate_cases.py) don't substitute for a live execution test. The gate uses claude-haiku-4-5, the same model used as acceptance criterion during case generation — it tests whether hard cases are still hard at experiment time, not just at generation time. validate_cases.py already flagged only 2/12 hard critique cases have domain expertise keyword signals, making the live gate more important, not less.
alternatives: Skip gate on grounds that proxy_mean-derived labels are empirically grounded — rejected because proxy_mean was measured at generation time on Sonnet; haiku-4-5 gate at experiment time catches calibration drift and is the designed acceptance criterion

description: No automated sync between plugins/ml-journal/ source and ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ — cache drifts silently after edits
context: Every edit to plugins/ml-journal/ requires a manual cache sync. There is no guard or post-edit step that enforces this. The recurring failure mode: source files change, cache is stale, skill behavior doesn't match source. A useful fix would be a post-edit step in any ml-journal change plan: sync modified files to cache and verify with grep.

description: Use os.getppid() as session_id fallback in journal_log.py
rationale: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not exposed to subprocess environments so it always returns null; PPID is the claude process PID which is stable for the lifetime of a session and unique across concurrent sessions
alternatives: Time-scoped queries (--since Nh) — conflates concurrent sessions; session file written at init — clobbered by concurrent sessions; drop session grouping entirely

Closed as intentional — PEER_REVIEW_R2.md omitted from required artifacts by design
Peer review depth for v5: single Opus round, conditional Round 2 not required
rationale: v5 is a meta-evaluation of the ml-lab debate protocol, not a primary research contribution going to publication. The peer review step exists to catch report-writing errors, not to validate novel claims. A single research-reviewer (Opus) pass is sufficient for that purpose. Round 2 (research-reviewer-lite) remains available as a conditional safety valve if Round 1 surfaces MAJOR issues, but it is not a required artifact and not verified in Phase 11.

One-off allow entries retained intentionally — they serve as the fallback permission set once bypassPermissions is removed. Removing them now would require rebuilding them post-experiment.
implications: After v5 experiment completes: remove permissions.defaultMode: bypassPermissions, skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt, AND the entire permissions.deny block from settings.local.json. The deny list is a safety floor that only makes sense under bypassPermissions — once normal approval prompts are restored, it is redundant.

message: chore: snapshot v5 Phase 6 raw outputs — 1650 files, isolation clean
diff_summary: Commits Phase 6 full benchmark run — 1650 raw output files (110 cases × 5 conditions × 3 runs). Includes schema repair scripts for missing multiround fields and list-type raw field conversions; isolation check clean across all 330 isolated debate runs.

description: validate_raw_schema.py had no stub exemption: forced_multiround not_applicable_difficulty stubs failed schema validation because validator applied full multiround field checks regardless of stub status

111 multiround/forced_multiround output files missing structured metadata fields: points_resolved, points_force_resolved, point_resolution_rate at top level; forced_multiround rounds entries missing verdict/points_resolved/points_open per-round snapshots. Root cause: batch agents did not serialize these fields. Repaired post-hoc with conservative inference (final verdict propagated to all rounds; points_resolved=len(issues_found) for critique_wins, 0 for defense_wins). Phase 10.5 hollow-round detection unreliable for repaired files — annotated with schema_repair_note.

30 isolated_debate output files (10 cases from batch 5) stored critic_raw/defender_raw as single-element lists instead of strings. Root cause: agent serialized agent output as a list wrapper. Repaired by joining list elements into string. check_isolation.py also crashed on None-type critic_raw; fixed with or-empty-string guard.

eval_scenario_428 and eval_scenario_524 forced_multiround files: debate_rounds field was accidentally assigned the rounds list content instead of an integer count; rounds field was None. Root cause: agent assigned the wrong variable to the field. Repaired by extracting rounds data from debate_rounds, building proper per-round snapshots, and setting debate_rounds = len(extracted_list).
v5 Phase 7 null result caused by orchestrator-mediated issues_found contaminating IDR/IDP scoring across all conditions including baseline
what_failed: H1 fc_lift = +0.0098 (threshold +0.10). All conditions including baseline scored near ceiling (0.96-0.97), producing near-zero lift.
root_cause: Two-layer data contract mismatch: (1) self_debate_poc.py computes IDR/IDP via literal string matching against must_find_issue_ids using the issues_found list from raw output files. (2) issues_found was populated by the batch orchestrator agents — not a neutral scorer. Orchestrators read the critic free-text and attributed issues_found generously across all conditions, including baseline. A baseline assessment that implicitly described the flaw received issues_found: [issue_001] from the orchestrator, giving baseline IDR = 1.0. ARCH-1 proxy scoring used a separate scorer LLM for semantic matching, which measured genuine detection difficulty from free-text responses. That signal correctly differentiated hard/medium cases. The literal-match rubric bypassed that signal by trusting the orchestrator attribution instead.
contributing_factors: Batch agents never saw must_find_issue_ids (correctly excluded per protocol), so they inferred issues_found from their own reading of critic_raw — which introduced systematic upward bias toward 1.0 across all conditions equally.
remediation: Implement a re-scoring script that reads critic_raw from each output file, runs semantic matching against must_find_issue_ids descriptions, and recomputes IDR/IDP independently of the orchestrator-attributed issues_found. Re-run Phase 7 on re-scored data.

description: Orchestrator answer-key leakage in issues_found extraction: batch agents loaded full benchmark_cases_verified.json (needed for task_prompt), giving orchestrator context-window access to scoring_targets.must_find_issue_ids and planted_issues descriptions. Phase 6 instructions explicitly told the orchestrator to extract issues_found by checking which must_find_issue_ids appear in critic output — meaning the orchestrator scored outputs with the answer key in hand. This applies to ALL conditions including baseline: a baseline assessment that described a flaw in its own words received issues_found: [issue_001] from the orchestrator because the orchestrator knew issue_001 described that flaw. Sub-agents (ml-critic, ml-defender, baseline assessor) were clean — they only saw task_prompt. The scoring step was not. This collapsed the IDR/IDP distinction between conditions and made fc_lift ~0 regardless of actual agent quality.
ADDENDUM to c82df212 — exact leakage mechanism, verdict integrity assessment, and re-scoring scope
Re-scorer architecture: async OpenRouter calls with asyncio.Semaphore(100), not parallel subagents
rationale: The re-scorer task is pure I/O — send text + flaw description, await yes/no response. Subagents add context-loading and tool-schema overhead for no benefit. Async API calls with a concurrency cap achieve higher throughput with simpler code. OPENROUTER_API_KEY is already configured; OpenAI async client against OpenRouter is the established pattern in this pipeline (same as ARCH-1 batch generation).
alternatives: Parallel subagents (10 agents × 120 files each) — rejected: subagent overhead is unjustified for a fixed single-inference task; sequential single script — rejected: too slow at 6000 calls

message: fix: correct plugin names from claude-ml-* to ml-* to match marketplace
diff_summary: Renames both plugin.json name fields from the stale claude-ml-lab/claude-ml-journal prefix to ml-lab/ml-journal, matching the marketplace.json source of truth. Updates all install commands and reinstall references across README, slides, and skill files.

message: feat: v5 Phase 7 re-run — honest IDR/IDP via isolated rescorer, H1 confirmed FAILS
diff_summary: Adds isolated semantic re-scorer (rescore_idr_idp.py) that fixes orchestrator answer-key leakage in IDR/IDP; patches self_debate_poc.py to consume rescored values; re-runs Phase 7 scoring — honest fc_lift=+0.0097 (CI includes 0), H1 still FAILS.

description: Orchestrator answer-key leakage in IDR/IDP fixed via isolated semantic re-scorer
v5 H1 honest result — fc_lift=+0.0097, H1 FAILS (not a leakage artifact)
Ensemble IDR empty rationales are a schema difference, not a scorer failure
implications: Rescored IDR/IDP results are trustworthy across all conditions. The -0.105 ensemble IDR drop vs baseline is a structural effect of the 2/3 majority vote threshold, not scorer leniency or failure. Ensemble scores are valid and audit-ready once the downstream read path is fixed.
source: Stratified rationale audit of v5_rescored_idr_idp.json — all 240 ensemble files showed 100% empty rationale strings at detail level; inspection of raw JSON revealed rationales are nested inside assessor_results[N].rationale rather than at detail.rationale (flat schema used by all other conditions)

description: Audit path reads detail.rationale directly — silently misses ensemble rationales in idr_detail
context: Discovered during rescorer trustworthiness audit (stratified rationale check). Ensemble idr_detail entries use schema {found, assessor_results: [{found, rationale}], majority_threshold} while all other conditions use flat {found, rationale}. Any code reading detail.get('rationale','') returns empty string for all ensemble entries, creating false impression that ensemble judgments are unauditable. Fix: detect ensemble entries by presence of assessor_results key and unpack accordingly. Scorer output is correct; bug is in downstream audit/reporting code only.

description: forced_multiround protocol gap — null verdicts on eval_scenario_381, 411, 616
DC metric fully redundant with FVC in v5 — mean_abs_delta=0.0 all conditions
difficulty labels do not predict rubric performance — FM hard-only scoping loses theoretical grounding
Stop rescorer trustworthiness assessment after checks #1 and #2
rationale: Stratum A/B rationale audit confirmed scorer is making precise, legitimate discriminations. Condition IDR gradient is internally coherent with H1 failure. Ensemble empty rationales resolved as schema difference (discovery 6bc7f8a1) — scores are valid. Sufficient confidence in rescored IDR/IDP results without proceeding to cross-model agreement or calibration probes.
alternatives: Cross-model agreement (Cohen's kappa via --model flag rerun on a sample), calibration probes with synthetic known-answer texts
